steps.title: Infinitely many solutions mean equations are the same line
steps.content: Two linear equations have infinitely many solutions if they represent the same line. This happens when one equation is a scalar multiple of the other—all coefficients and the constant are scaled by the same factor.

steps.title: Check choice C for scalar multiples
steps.content:
For choice C:
  • Equation 1: $x + 2y = 12$
  • Equation 2: $4x + 8y = 48$
Multiply equation 1 by 4:
$$4(x + 2y) = 4(12) \implies 4x + 8y = 48$$
This is exactly equation 2, so both equations describe the same line and have infinitely many solutions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
